$comment: Season dossier pack S06, The Temple Wars (Horsepower War act). Season-scoped coverage for the slipstream season: the tow as law, wings trimmed to nothing, the power cathedrals as battlegrounds, and the quiet file of frights being kept. Ids are stable rendezvous keys, never rename. Read-side only: editing this file changes display, never replay.

templates.headline: THE TEMPLE WARS: the tow is law now, and the wings come off
templates.deck: A season that will be settled by the length of a nose
templates.summary: Season six opens with every garage chasing the low-drag dare at the power cathedrals.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The winter belonged to the drag men: every garage in the paddock spent it shaving wing, chasing the trimmed-out philosophy that terrorised the fast circuits last autumn. They are calling them the temple wars now, and the temples are the straights where the slipstream decides everything.
sections.context: The arithmetic of the season is simple and savage: minimum downforce, maximum terminal speed, and a car that dances on the straights and prays in the corners. {pool:paddockBuzz}
sections.close: Sixteen rounds, and the big ones will be settled by a car's length in the final run to the line.

templates.headline: {subject} WINS[[?venue: at {venue}]], slingshot out of the final tow
templates.deck: The temple settles its argument by a nose

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Patience, mirrors, and one perfect run to the line: {subject} rode the tow like a drafting lesson and struck when the road ran out. The temple wars make winners exactly this way, and the grandstand has found a new patron saint. {pool:swCrowd}

templates.headline: {subject} on the podium[[?venue: at {venue}]], out of the three-wide scramble
templates.deck: A podium auctioned off at terminal speed

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The tow does not hand out podiums, it auctions them, and {subject} bid highest when the pack came through three abreast. The margin at the line was a coat of paint and a full commitment. {pool:swCrowd}

templates.headline: POLE for {subject}[[?venue: at {venue}]], wings trimmed to nothing
templates.deck: The low-drag dare, taken to its ragged edge

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The setup sheet said minimum wing and maximum nerve, and {subject} signed it in full. Pole[[?venue: at {venue}]] belongs to the bravest right foot in the paddock and the car that danced the straights without a wobble. {pool:swBoard}
sections.close: Sunday will ask the corners to forgive what the straights encouraged. The tow will be watching.

templates.headline: {subject} leads the temple wars, above the theatre
templates.summary: {subject} took the championship lead in season six.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} While the showmen traded paint at terminal speed, {subject} banked the arithmetic, and tonight the big board files a new name at the summit. The temple wars reward the brave on Sundays; they crown the patient in autumn. {pool:swBoard}
sections.close: The cathedrals still have their loudest rounds to come. The lead must be defended in the tow, where everyone is faster than the car ahead.

templates.headline: {subject} parks it[[?venue: at {venue}]], the straight claims another engine
templates.deck: A powerplant spent at terminal speed

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The tow demands full throttle for longer than engines were built to give, and today the account came due under {subject}[[?venue: at {venue}]]. A puff, a silence, a long walk back past the grandstand's sympathy. {pool:wrenchNote}
sections.close: The engine men will count the laps it gave against the laps it owed. The difference is the season.

templates.headline: {subject} crashes out[[?venue: at {venue}]], the trimmed car bites back
templates.deck: A wing-stripped machine finds the corner it was praying about

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} A car trimmed to nothing gives its driver speed on the straight and a very long moment to think in the corner. {subject} got the moment and not the corner[[?venue: at {venue}]], and the temple wars collected their due. The driver walked away; the frights file gets one entry thicker. {pool:swCrowd}
sections.close: Somewhere in an office, a man drafting wing regulations underlines a sentence.
